Based on the search results, I can provide you with comprehensive information about Datadog AWS ECS on EC2 Integration and the events collected (イベント 収集).

## Datadog AWS ECS on EC2 Integration Overview

Datadog collects ECS metrics automatically from CloudWatch using the Amazon ECS Datadog integration and expands on those metrics by querying the ECS API for ECS events, tags, and the status of container instances, tasks, and services.

## Events Collected (イベント 収集)

### Event Filtering
To reduce noise, the Amazon ECS integration is automatically set up to include only events that contain the following words: drain, error, fail, insufficient memory, pending, reboot, terminate.(no content)To remove the inclusion list and receive all events from your Datadog Amazon ECS integration, you need to reach out to Datadog support.

### Data Collection Methods
Datadog automatically collects ECS metrics from CloudWatch and can expand on those metrics by querying the ECS API for ECS events, tags, and the status of container instances, tasks, and services.

## Integration Setup
To monitor your ECS containers and tasks, deploy the Datadog Agent as a container once on each EC2 instance in your ECS cluster by creating a task definition for the Datadog Agent container and deploying it as a daemon service.

## Agent Deployment
The Datadog Agent is open source software that collects metrics, request traces, logs, and process data from your ECS environment, and sends this information to Datadog. The Agent runs inside your ECS cluster, gathering resource metrics as well as metrics from containerized web servers, message brokers, and other services.

## Log Collection
Once enabled, the Datadog Agent container collects the logs emitted from the other application containers on the same host as itself, limited to the logs emitted to the stdout and stderr log stream when using the default or json-file logging driver.

## Additional Features
Datadog's Autodiscovery can be used in conjunction with ECS and Docker to automatically discover and monitor running tasks in your environment.

The integration provides comprehensive monitoring capabilities for ECS on EC2, automatically collecting filtered events and metrics while allowing for customization through API queries and additional configuration options.

content.text:
検索結果に基づいて、DatadogのAWS ECS on EC2 Integrationで収集されるイベントについて説明します：

## 収集されるイベント

DatadogのAWS ECS Integrationは以下のイベントを収集します：

### イベントフィルタリング
デフォルトでは、ノイズを減らすために以下のキーワードを含むイベントのみが自動的に収集されます：
- **drain** (ドレイン)
- **error** (エラー)
- **fail** (失敗)
- **insufficient memory** (メモリ不足)
- **pending** (待機中)
- **reboot** (再起動)
- **terminate** (終了)

### データ収集方法
1. **CloudWatch** からECSメトリクスを自動収集
2. **ECS API** を使用してECSイベント、タグ、コンテナインスタンス・タスク・サービスのステータスを収集

### イベントフィルタの変更
すべてのECSイベントを受信したい場合は、Datadogサポートに連絡してインクルージョンリストを削除する必要があります。

## 根拠となるリンク
- [Amazon ECS on EC2 Integration](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/amazon-ecs/)
- [Amazon ECS Data Collection](https://docs.datadoghq.com/containers/amazon_ecs/data_collected/)
- [Monitoring ECS with Datadog](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/monitoring-ecs-with-datadog/)

つまり、主にECSクラスターの運用に関わる重要なイベント（エラー、メモリ不足、再起動など）が自動的にフィルタリングされて収集されています。
